prompt: Vertical portrait 9:16 high-resolution atlas world map for a Call of Cthulhu 1920s Lovecraftian cosmic horror RPG setting. Terrain type: single_continent. Draw one immense fictional continent in aged parchment and 1920s investigative cartography style, with subtle hand-annotation marks but no readable text. Include a New England-like coastline, Arkham-style town districts, Miskatonic river delta, fog-shrouded rocky hills, old colonial architecture, lighthouse coasts, lonely fishing villages, forbidden library sites, cult meeting ruins, ancient standing stones, sealed cave systems, strange marshes, old rail lines, black pine forests, decaying mansions, stormy harbors, and hints of sleeping things beneath earth and sea. The continent must span many climates and biomes: foggy northeastern coast, cold mountains, deep forests, haunted wetlands, bleak moors, arid interior badlands, volcanic blacklands, river valleys, storm coasts, and isolated icy north. Coastline must be realistic and complex with peninsulas, bays, gulfs, straits, broken shores, curved coasts, river deltas, fjords, barrier islands, sea caves, and varied landforms. Include irregular offshore islands in uneven clusters, all different sizes and shapes, some with lighthouses, standing stones, ruined chapels, fishing ports, and forbidden dig sites. Large detailed atlas composition, creeping cosmic dread, sepia ink, no readable labels, no legible text, no logos, no modern satellite style.
